analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.subtitle: Financial totals use <1>GET /api/v1/admin/analytics/financial?range=…</1>. Charts fill when the API returns time series or category breakdown; otherwise an empty state is shown. Leaderboards use <2>GET …/analytics/leaderboards</2>.

revenueTrend.title: Revenue trend ({{range}})
revenueTrend.description: Daily series when provided by the API.
revenueTrend.empty: No revenue-by-day series in this response.

revenueByCategory.title: Revenue by category
revenueByCategory.description: Populated when the API returns a breakdown array.
revenueByCategory.empty: No category breakdown in this response.

usersByRole.title: Users by role
usersByRole.description: Derived from <1>GET /dashboard/counters</1> when the API returns only flat totals (role split may be partial until the backend adds breakdown fields).

eventsByStatus.title: Events by status
eventsByStatus.description: Mock-only — same note as users by role.

leaderboards.noEvents: No events in this leaderboard response.
leaderboards.noOrganizers: No organizer rows in this response.

activity.title: Activity & audit
activity.subtitle: <1>GET /api/v1/admin/admin-actions</1> lists the action catalog; <2>POST /api/v1/admin/admin-actions</2> records a row (see guide — body uses <3>action_kind</3>, not arbitrary keys). <4>GET …/audit-logs</4> and <5>GET …/audit-logs/{id}</5> for detail (IP, user-agent, changes when the API provides them).

recordAction.title: Record admin action (POST)
recordAction.description: JSON body must match the server contract: required <1>action_kind</1> (enum), optional <2>target_type</2>, <3>target_id</3>, <4>summary</4>, <5>metadata</5>. Open the guide if you are unsure.

notify.jsonRootMustBeObject: Root JSON value must be an object.

auditLog.title: Audit log
auditLog.noChanges: No structured changes on this entry.

guide.title: Recording work on the activity timeline
guide.subtitle: POST <1>/api/v1/admin/admin-actions</1> — append a narrative row for audit, separate from domain endpoints that change real state.

inOneSentence.heading: In one sentence
inOneSentence.body: Each POST appends one row to <1>admin_actions</1>: which admin (from your session) performed which <2>kind</2> of action, optionally pointed at a target, with a short <3>summary</3> and optional structured <4>metadata</4>.

whoIsAdmin.heading: Who is the admin?
whoIsAdmin.body: <1>admin_user_id</1> is taken from the authenticated admin. Do <2>not</2> send it in the JSON body.

laravelVsDb.heading: Laravel vs database (important)
laravelVsDb.p1: The API only validates <1>action_kind</1> as <2>required string</2> — there is <3>no</3> PHP whitelist of allowed values.
laravelVsDb.p2: The <1>admin_actions</1> table stores <2>action_kind</2> as a MySQL <3>ENUM</3> with a <4>fixed list</4>. If you send any other string, PHP may accept the request but the insert can fail at the DB layer (often a <5>500</5>).
laravelVsDb.p3: Practical rule: only use one of the allowed enum values below. Free-form kinds (e.g. <1>manual_phone_call</1>) need a schema change on the server.

allowedKinds.heading: Allowed action_kind values
allowedKinds.description: Pick the value that best matches the story; put nuance in <1>summary</1> / <2>metadata</2>.

fields.heading: Fields the handler reads
fields.actionKind: Required. Must be one of the enum values above (DB-enforced), even though Laravel only checks “string”.
fields.targetType: Optional. Free text for your audit trail (e.g. <1>user</1>, <2>event</2>), max <3>80</3> characters in the DB. The API does not verify the target exists.
fields.summary: Optional narrative, max <1>500</1> characters — use this for the human-readable story.
fields.metadata: Optional JSON object (your own keys/shapes), as long as it serializes to valid JSON and fits storage. Extra top-level keys in the request body are <1>ignored</1> unless the backend is extended to read them.

whenToUse.heading: When to use this
whenToUse.item1: You already performed the real change via another endpoint, but want a clear line on the timeline.
whenToUse.item2: You did offline / manual work (phone, legal review) that should still appear under admin activity.
whenToUse.footer: New rows appear when you refresh <1>GET /api/v1/admin/admin-actions</1> (this page lists the catalog; your timeline / list view may differ by product wiring).

minimalBody.heading: Minimal valid body

richerExample.heading: Richer example (201 response)
richerExample.description: The API returns the created row under <1>data</1> (including <2>id</2>, <3>admin_user_id</3>, <4>created_at</4>, etc.).
